checks.a_analysis: The output follows the source document's text content accurately. No fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or sections were detected. The metadata title and image alt text are acceptable pipeline additions.
There are no fabricated phone numbers, emails, or URLs. The URL present in the source is omitted rather than misstated (addressed in C5).
All pages (1 of 1) and all major sections (Header, Agenda Table, Dates, Website, Committee Reports Table) are present in the output.
The output contains two tables. The first table (Agenda) has 9 rows (1 header + 8 data), matching the source. The second table (Committee Reports) has 6 rows, matching the source's list of 6 items.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The URL for the GICC Website is completely missing from the output. In the paragraph element with reading_order 6, the label 'GICC Website: ' is present, but the actual URL 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc' is absent. Additionally, there is a gap in the reading_order (element 7 is missing), suggesting the link was dropped during conversion. As this URL is the primary information of that section, its omission is major.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Excluding the missing URL noted in C5, the text content matches the source document exactly, including names, times, and agenda item descriptions.

checks.a_analysis: The content from the source PDF is represented accurately without duplication. No paragraphs, sections, or table rows are repeated in the output JSON.

The heading hierarchy is logical and sequential (H1 to H2 to H3). The organization name is H1, the agenda title/date is H2, and subsequent section headers like 'Meeting Dates' and 'Committee Reports' are H3. No heading levels are skipped.

The conversion uses a table to represent the 'Committee Reports' list at the bottom of the document. However, the first row of actual data ('Statewide Mapping Advisory' / 'Paul Badr') is incorrectly identified as a header row ('is_header_row': true), which misidentifies data as structural metadata for the table.

The reading order follows the source document's logical flow. The logo has been moved to the final element (reading_order 10), which is an acceptable adjustment for screen reader accessibility to prioritize the document's H1 title.

In Agenda Item 4, the nested sub-list (items a, b, and c) is flattened into a single text block within the table cell. It is not represented as a nested list structure, causing a loss of structural hierarchy.
checks.b_evidence_source:
a. Management and Operations Committee
b. Federal Interagency Committee
c. Technical Advisory Committee
checks.b_evidence_output:
"text": "4. Committee Bylaws Updates [Council Vote Required]
a. Management and Operations Committee
b. Federal Interagency Committee
c. Technical Advisory Committee"

checks.a_analysis: The URL 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc' visible in the source PDF is missing from the output text content entirely. The paragraph only contains the label 'GICC Website: '.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the HTML document closely follows the source PDF. The main body content—headings, meeting information, agenda table, and committee reports—is presented in a logical single-column flow consistent with the source. The addition of a 'Contents' navigation sidebar is an expected accessibility feature. Although the NC GICC logo has been moved to the bottom of the page in the HTML output, the overall layout effectively preserves the source document's information hierarchy.

The tables in the HTML output are rendered correctly and are highly legible. The Agenda table (columns: TIME, AGENDA ITEM, GICC MEMBER or GUEST) uses clear borders and distinct headers. The 'Committee Reports' section, which appeared as a bulleted list with right-aligned names in the PDF, has been converted into a clean two-column table in the HTML. Both tables maintain alignment, and content is not overlapping or truncated.

The primary meaningful image from the source PDF, the 'NC GICC' logo, is successfully rendered in the HTML output. While its position has moved to the bottom of the document, the image itself is intact and clearly visible.

There is no visual duplication of body content in the HTML output. While document titles and section headings appear in both the 'Contents' sidebar and the main body, this is an intentional and expected navigation feature for accessible documents. No paragraphs, tables, or sections are repeated within the main body text.
